name: Jira
description: Issue and project tracking for software teams. Create and manage issues, run JQL searches, and track sprint progress.
author: OpenJiuwen
desc_mk: Jira is a powerful issue and project tracking tool used by software development teams. Use this plugin to manage issues, run JQL searches, and track projects. **Note:** Replace `your-domain` in the API prefix with your Atlassian domain. Get your API token at [Atlassian Account Settings](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens). The Authorization header uses Base64-encoded `email:api_token` (Environment variable: JIRA_AUTH_TOKEN).

Authorization.description: Base64-encoded email:api_token for Jira authentication (Environment variable: JIRA_AUTH_TOKEN)

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: Search for projects using pagination and optional query filters

query.description: Filter projects by name or key containing this text

orderBy.description: Order results by field: category, issueCount, key, lastIssueUpdatedTime, name, or owner

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 50, max 50)

startAt.description: Index of the first result to return (0-based)

tools.name: Get Project
tools.description: Return the project details for a given project ID or key

projectIdOrKey.description: The project ID or project key of the project to retrieve

tools.name: Get Issue
tools.description: Return the details of a single issue in Jira

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to retrieve (e.g. PROJ-123)

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return. Use * for all fields or -field to exclude

expand.description: Comma-separated list of additional fields to expand in the response

tools.name: Create Issue
tools.description: Create a new issue or subtask in Jira

fields.description: Issue fields object. Required: project ({"key": "PROJ"}), summary, issuetype ({"name": "Bug"}). Optional: description, assignee ({"accountId": "xxx"}), priority ({"name": "High"}), labels (array). Example: {"project": {"key": "PROJ"}, "summary": "Issue title", "issuetype": {"name": "Bug"}}

tools.name: Update Issue
tools.description: Edit an existing Jira issue's fields

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to update (e.g. PROJ-123)

fields.description: Issue fields to update. Example: {"summary": "New title", "description": "Updated description", "priority": {"name": "Low"}, "labels": ["bug", "backend"]}

tools.name: Delete Issue
tools.description: Delete an issue permanently from Jira

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to delete (e.g. PROJ-123)

tools.name: Search Issues (JQL)
tools.description: Search for issues using Jira Query Language (JQL)

jql.description: JQL query string. Example: project = PROJ AND status = 'In Progress' ORDER BY created DESC

maxResults.description: Maximum number of issues to return (default 50, max 100)

startAt.description: Index of the first issue to return (0-based)

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return for each issue

expand.description: Comma-separated list of additional fields to expand

tools.name: Add Comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an existing Jira issue

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to comment on (e.g. PROJ-123)

body.description: Comment body in Atlassian Document Format (ADF). Example: {"type": "doc", "version": 1, "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Your comment here"}]}]}

tools.name: List Comments
tools.description: Return all comments for an issue

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to get comments for (e.g. PROJ-123)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of comments to return

startAt.description: Index of the first comment to return (0-based)

tools.name: Transition Issue
tools.description: Transition an issue to a different workflow status (e.g. move to In Progress, Done)

issueIdOrKey.description: The ID or key of the issue to transition (e.g. PROJ-123)

transition.description: Transition object with the target status ID. Get available transition IDs via GET /issue/{key}/transitions. Example: {"id": "31"}
